# CDC Vessel Sanitation Program — Cruise Ship Outbreaks 2026 (Current List)

## Overview

As of **June 1, 2026** (the page's last update date), the CDC Vessel Sanitation Program (VSP) has recorded **5 gastrointestinal (GI) illness outbreaks** on cruise ships within its jurisdiction during 2026. The data was obtained directly from the CDC's official VSP outbreak updates page at `https://www.cdc.gov/vessel-sanitation/cruise-ship-outbreaks/index.html`.

## 2026 Outbreaks — Complete List with Detail

### 1. National Geographic Sea Bird (Lindblad Expeditions)
- **Voyage dates:** May 26–31, 2026
- **Voyage number:** SB32621
- **Date reported to VSP:** May 28, 2026
- **Passengers ill:** 9 of 66 (**13.6%**)
- **Crew ill:** 3 of 24 (**12.5%**)
- **Predominant symptoms:** Vomiting, diarrhea
- **Causative agent:** Unknown (no samples provided)
- **Response:** VSP remotely monitored; ship increased cleaning/disinfection, isolated ill individuals
- **Page last updated:** June 1, 2026

### 2. Caribbean Princess (Princess Cruises)
- **Voyage dates:** April 28–May 11, 2026
- **Voyage number:** B612
- **Date reported to VSP:** May 7, 2026
- **Passengers ill:** 145 of 3,116 (**4.7%**)
- **Crew ill:** 15 of 1,131 (**1.3%**)
- **Predominant symptoms:** Diarrhea, vomiting
- **Causative agent:** **Norovirus**
- **Response:** VSP conducted a **field response** (environmental assessment and outbreak investigation); stool specimens collected
- **Page last updated:** May 11, 2026

### 3. Oceania Insignia (Oceania Cruises)
- **Voyage dates:** April 7–14, 2026
- **Voyage number:** INS260404
- **Date reported to VSP:** April 13, 2026
- **Passengers ill:** 19 of 633 (**3.0%**)
- **Crew ill:** 3 of 394 (**0.8%**)
- **Predominant symptoms:** Diarrhea
- **Causative agent:** ***E. coli***
- **Response:** VSP remotely monitored; stool specimens collected
- **Page last updated:** April 23, 2026

### 4. Star Princess (Princess Cruises)
- **Voyage dates:** March 7–14, 2026
- **Voyage number:** 4611
- **Date reported to VSP:** March 11, 2026
- **Passengers ill:** 141 of 4,307 (**3.3%**)
- **Crew ill:** 52 of 1,561 (**3.3%**)
- **Predominant symptoms:** Diarrhea, vomiting
- **Causative agent:** **Norovirus**
- **Response:** VSP conducted a **field response** (environmental assessment and outbreak investigation); stool specimens collected
- **Page last updated:** March 16, 2026

### 5. Seven Seas Mariner (Regent Seven Seas)
- **Voyage dates:** January 11–February 1, 2026
- **Voyage number:** MAR260111
- **Date reported to VSP:** January 29, 2026
- **Passengers ill:** 21 of 631 (**3.3%**)
- **Crew ill:** 6 of 458 (**1.3%**)
- **Predominant symptoms:** Diarrhea
- **Causative agent:** ***E. coli***
- **Response:** VSP remotely monitored; stool specimens collected
- **Page last updated:** March 3, 2026

## Summary Statistics for 2026 (Jan 1 – June 1)

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total outbreaks** | 5 |
| **Total passengers ill** | 335 |
| **Total crew ill** | 79 |
| **Total ill (all)** | 414 |
| **Norovirus outbreaks** | 2 (40%) |
| **E. coli outbreaks** | 2 (40%) |
| **Unknown agent** | 1 (20%) |
| **VSP field responses** | 2 (Caribbean Princess, Star Princess) |
| **VSP remote monitoring** | 3 |

## Causative Agent Breakdown
- **Norovirus**: 2 outbreaks (Caribbean Princess, Star Princess) — both Princess Cruises ships, both with large passenger counts
- **E. coli**: 2 outbreaks (Oceania Insignia, Seven Seas Mariner) — both on smaller luxury ships
- **Unknown**: 1 outbreak (National Geographic Sea Bird) — no samples were provided by ill passengers/crew

## Comparison with 2025
For context, 2025 saw **at least 20 outbreaks** (the list on the earlier outbreaks page was extensive). Key differences:
- 2025 was heavily dominated by **norovirus** (the vast majority of identified agents)
- 2026 has seen a notable proportion of **E. coli** outbreaks (2 of 5, or 40%)
- 2026's pace (5 outbreaks in ~5 months) is substantially lower than 2025's pace (~20 outbreaks in 12 months, or ~1.7/month vs. ~1.0/month in 2026)
- Princess Cruises appears twice on the 2026 list (Caribbean Princess and Star Princess)

## CDC Reporting Criteria
The CDC posts outbreaks when both conditions are met:
1. The ship is under VSP jurisdiction (voyages including both U.S. and foreign ports)
2. 3% or more of passengers **or** crew report symptoms of GI illness to the ship's medical staff

## Highest-Impact Outbreaks in 2026
- **Largest by absolute numbers:** Caribbean Princess (160 total ill: 145 passengers + 15 crew)
- **Highest passenger attack rate:** National Geographic Sea Bird (13.6% of passengers)
- **Highest crew attack rate:** National Geographic Sea Bird (12.5% of crew)
- **Largest ship affected:** Star Princess (5,868 total passengers + crew)

key_facts: As of June 1, 2026, the CDC VSP lists 5 cruise ship GI illness outbreaks for 2026
Causative agents: Norovirus (2 outbreaks), E. coli (2 outbreaks), Unknown (1 outbreak)
Total passengers ill across all 5 outbreaks: 335; total crew ill: 79; combined: 414
National Geographic Sea Bird (Lindblad Expeditions) had the highest attack rate: 13.6% passengers, 12.5% crew (May 26-31, 2026)
Caribbean Princess (Princess Cruises) had the most cases: 145 passengers + 15 crew ill from norovirus (Apr 28-May 11, 2026)
Star Princess (Princess Cruises) had 141 passengers + 52 crew ill from norovirus (Mar 7-14, 2026)
Oceania Insignia had 19 passengers + 3 crew ill from E. coli (Apr 7-14, 2026)
Seven Seas Mariner (Regent Seven Seas) had 21 passengers + 6 crew ill from E. coli (Jan 11-Feb 1, 2026)
CDC conducted in-person field responses for Caribbean Princess and Star Princess outbreaks
2025 had at least 20 outbreaks for the full year, making 2026's pace (~5 in 5 months) lower
Princess Cruises had 2 of the 5 outbreaks in 2026
CDC reporting threshold: 3% or more of passengers or crew reporting GI illness symptoms
